title: peucedanum terebinthaceum f. aciculare
intro: Peucedanum terebinthaceum f. aciculare, also known as fern-leaved hemlock, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Peucedanum terebinthaceum f. aciculare include its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and reduce fever. Traditionally, it has been prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
